title: Theatre Square
description: Given input data, design an efficient algorithm to resolve "Theatre Square" using standard parameters. Optimize for both memory usage and execution time.

solutions.python:
import math

def solve():
    n, m, a = map(int, input().split())
    count_n = math.ceil(n / a)
    count_m = math.ceil(m / a)
    print(count_n * count_m)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
solutions.explanation: This is the solution guide for the problem "Theatre Square". It leverages the "Modular Arithmetic" core algorithm pattern. We initialize pointers/structures, traverse the dataset, perform state transitions, and return the calculated result. The algorithm achieves optimal time and space complexity.

title: Valid Sudoku
description: Given input data, design an efficient algorithm to resolve "Valid Sudoku" using standard parameters. Optimize for both memory usage and execution time.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def isValidSudoku(self, board: List[List[str]]) -> bool:
        cols = collections.defaultdict(set)
        rows = collections.defaultdict(set)
        squares = collections.defaultdict(set)
        for r in range(9):
            for c in range(9):
                val = board[r][c]
                if val == ".":
                    continue
                if (val in rows[r] or 
                    val in cols[c] or 
                    val in squares[(r // 3, c // 3)]):
                    return False
                cols[c].add(val)
                rows[r].add(val)
                squares[(r // 3, c // 3)].add(val)
        return True
solutions.explanation: This is the solution guide for the problem "Valid Sudoku". It leverages the "Sparse Matrix" core algorithm pattern. We initialize pointers/structures, traverse the dataset, perform state transitions, and return the calculated result. The algorithm achieves optimal time and space complexity.

title: Word Search
description: Given input data, design an efficient algorithm to resolve "Word Search" using standard parameters. Optimize for both memory usage and execution time.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def exist(self, board: List[List[str]], word: str) -> bool:
        rows, cols = len(board), len(board[0])
        path = set()
        
        def dfs(r, c, i):
            if i == len(word):
                return True
            if (r < 0 or c < 0 or 
                r >= rows or c >= cols or 
                word[i] != board[r][c] or 
                (r, c) in path):
                return False
                
            path.add((r, c))
            res = (dfs(r + 1, c, i + 1) or
                   dfs(r - 1, c, i + 1) or
                   dfs(r, c + 1, i + 1) or
                   dfs(r, c - 1, i + 1))
            path.remove((r, c))
            return res
            
        for r in range(rows):
            for c in range(cols):
                if dfs(r, c, 0): return True
        return False
solutions.explanation: This is the solution guide for the problem "Word Search". It leverages the "Backtracking" core algorithm pattern. We initialize pointers/structures, traverse the dataset, perform state transitions, and return the calculated result. The algorithm achieves optimal time and space complexity.

title: Subsets
description: Given input data, design an efficient algorithm to resolve "Subsets" using standard parameters. Optimize for both memory usage and execution time.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def subsets(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[List[int]]:
        res = []
        subset = []
        def dfs(i):
            if i >= len(nums):
                res.append(subset.copy())
                return
            subset.append(nums[i])
            dfs(i + 1)
            subset.pop()
            dfs(i + 1)
        dfs(0)
        return res
solutions.explanation: This is the solution guide for the problem "Subsets". It leverages the "Backtracking" core algorithm pattern. We initialize pointers/structures, traverse the dataset, perform state transitions, and return the calculated result. The algorithm achieves optimal time and space complexity.

title: N-Queens
description: Given input data, design an efficient algorithm to resolve "N-Queens" using standard parameters. Optimize for both memory usage and execution time.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def solveNQueens(self, n: int) -> List[List[str]]:
        col = set()
        posDiag = set() # (r + c)
        negDiag = set() # (r - c)
        
        res = []
        board = [["."] * n for _ in range(n)]
        
        def backtrack(r):
            if r == n:
                copy = ["".join(row) for row in board]
                res.append(copy)
                return
                
            for c in range(n):
                if c in col or (r + c) in posDiag or (r - c) in negDiag:
                    continue
                    
                col.add(c)
                posDiag.add(r + c)
                negDiag.add(r - c)
                board[r][c] = "Q"
                
                backtrack(r + 1)
                
                col.remove(c)
                posDiag.remove(r + c)
                negDiag.remove(r - c)
                board[r][c] = "."
                
        backtrack(0)
        return res
solutions.explanation: This is the solution guide for the problem "N-Queens". It leverages the "Backtracking" core algorithm pattern. We initialize pointers/structures, traverse the dataset, perform state transitions, and return the calculated result. The algorithm achieves optimal time and space complexity.
